perturbation.original_text: 4.8 The Parties shall consult as soon as possible but in any case within twenty (20) days of receiving an INVENTION NOTICE whether to proceed to obtain intellectual property protection on the disclosed JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY or whether to protect the same through other methods. CERES shall have the first option to pursue such protection in its best judgment in the joint names of both Parties. If CERES so elects by written notice within sixty (60) days of such initial consultation, CERES shall be the "ADMINISTERING PARTY" for the purposes of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.8 The Parties shall consult as soon as possible but in any case within twenty (20) days of receiving an INVENTION NOTICE whether to proceed to obtain intellectual property protection on the disclosed JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY or whether to protect the same through other methods. IGER shall have the first option to pursue such protection in its best judgment in the joint names of both Parties. If IGER so elects by written notice within sixty (60) days of such initial consultation, IGER shall be the "ADMINISTERING PARTY" for the purposes of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text grants CERES the first option to pursue intellectual property protection, while the modified text grants this option to IGER. This creates a direct contradiction regarding which party controls the initial process of securing IP protection for JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.9 CERES agrees to bear all legal expenses incurred by CERES as an ADMINISTERING PARTY in obtaining and maintaining patents and plant variety rights, U.S. and other, covering JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY.
perturbation.justification: The original text designates CERES as the party with the first option to pursue intellectual property protection and, consequently, as the 'ADMINISTERING PARTY.' The changed text, however, assigns this role to IGER. This directly contradicts section 4.9, which states that 'CERES agrees to bear all legal expenses incurred by CERES as an ADMINISTERING PARTY,' because the changed text reassigns the 'ADMINISTERING PARTY' role to IGER, potentially creating confusion about who is responsible for the legal expenses related to obtaining and maintaining patents. This falls under Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, as the change assigns different roles to different parties, which in turn creates a conflict on who is the 'ADMINISTERING PARTY'.

perturbation.original_text: 5.3.1 Subject to Articles 5.1 and 5.2, the Parties acknowledge and agree that CERES shall have the exclusive right, with the right to grant sublicenses to this right, to use and commercially exploit JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY (including but not limited to RELEASED VARIETIES) and OTHER RESEARCH RESULTS for any and all uses and fields, including, but not limited to, use in the COLLABORATION CROPS and crops other than the COLLABORATION CROPS. Any commercialization (including, but not limited to, the grant of commercialization licenses) of JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY and/or OTHER RESEARCH RESULTS shall result in CERES paying IGER a reasonable remuneration (whether as a royalty or in some other form as the Parties may agree) to be negotiated in good faith. The terms for remuneration will be specified, with respect to specific RESEARCH PROJECTS, JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY and/or OTHER RESEARCH RESULTS, either in the respective SCHEDULES or in other agreements to be executed by the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.3.1 Subject to Articles 5.1 and 5.2, the Parties acknowledge and agree that IGER shall have the exclusive right, with the right to grant sublicenses to this right, to use and commercially exploit JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY (including but not limited to RELEASED VARIETIES) and OTHER RESEARCH RESULTS for any and all uses and fields, including, but not limited to, use in the COLLABORATION CROPS and crops other than the COLLABORATION CROPS. Any commercialization (including, but not limited to, the grant of commercialization licenses) of JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY and/or OTHER RESEARCH RESULTS shall result in IGER paying CERES a reasonable remuneration (whether as a royalty or in some other form as the Parties may agree) to be negotiated in good faith. The terms for remuneration will be specified, with respect to specific RESEARCH PROJECTS, JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY and/or OTHER RESEARCH RESULTS, either in the respective SCHEDULES or in other agreements to be executed by the Parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text grants CERES the exclusive right to use and commercially exploit JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY while the modified text gives this right to IGER. This creates a contradiction regarding the commercialization rights of JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.3.2 Subject to Articles 5.1 and 5.2, IGER grants CERES an exclusive, world-wide license, with the right to grant sublicenses, to use and exploit commercially (a) IGER's interest in any JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY (including but not limited to RELEASED VARIETIES) and OTHER RESEARCH RESULTS; (b) IGER INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY and (c) when and only to the extent necessary for the commercialization or use of JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY and/or OTHER RESEARCH RESULTS and/or IGER INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY for the COLLABORATION CROPS, IGER BACKGROUND INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY, subject to paying IGER reasonable remuneration (whether as a royalty or in some other form as the Parties may agree) to be negotiated in good faith. Such license grants will be further specified in SCHEDULES to this Agreement and/or other agreements to be executed by the Parties. Unless otherwise agreed by both Parties, license agreements for non-transgenic RELEASED VARIETIES will be based on the Model License Agreement for Non-transgenic Varieties in EXHIBIT G or such other model license agreements as the Parties may agree upon.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants CERES the exclusive right to commercially exploit JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY. The changed text grants this same right to IGER, creating a direct contradiction. This directly conflicts with section 5.3.2, which grants CERES an exclusive worldwide license to use and exploit IGER's interest in JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY. If IGER already has the exclusive right as stated in the changed 5.3.1, then 5.3.2's grant of a license from IGER to CERES becomes illogical and contradictory, as IGER cannot grant a license for something CERES should already have rights to if the changed text is to be believed.

perturbation.original_text: 14.3.1 In case of termination on the basis of Article 14.1 (b) if CERES is the breaching Party, Article 14.1 (c) if CERES ceases substantially all activities in the COLLABORATION CROPS, Article 14.1 (d) (i), (ii) or (iii) or Article 14.1. (e) (iii), at or about the effective date of termination, the Parties will negotiate in good faith to reach agreement as to the rights to use and commercially exploit JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY not covered by any relevant license/commercialization agreement between the Parties, which rights will be addressed in one or more written agreements. If the Parties fail to reach agreement within ninety (90) days after the start of such negotiations, which shall be evidenced by written notice from one Party to the other initiating such negotiations, each Party shall have the non-exclusive right to use and commercially exploit JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY for any and all purposes, with the right to grant sublicenses, subject to the obligations of the first sentence of Article 14.3, provided that no licenses on any transgenes or transgenic technologies of the other Party shall be included or implied.
perturbation.changed_text: 14.3.1 In case of termination on the basis of Article 14.1 (b) if CERES is the breaching Party, Article 14.1 (c) if CERES ceases substantially all activities in the COLLABORATION CROPS, Article 14.1 (d) (i), (ii) or (iii) or Article 14.1. (e) (iii), at or about the effective date of termination, the Parties will negotiate in good faith to reach agreement as to the rights to use and commercially exploit JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY not covered by any relevant license/commercialization agreement between the Parties, which rights will be addressed in one or more written agreements. If the Parties fail to reach agreement within ninety (90) days after the start of such negotiations, which shall be evidenced by written notice from one Party to the other initiating such negotiations, CERES shall have the exclusive right to use and commercially exploit JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY for any and all purposes, with the right to grant sublicenses, subject to the obligations of the first sentence of Article 14.3, provided that no licenses on any transgenes or transgenic technologies of the other Party shall be included or implied.
perturbation.explanation: The original text grants each party the non-exclusive right to use and commercially exploit JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY under specific termination conditions, while the modified text grants CERES the exclusive right. This creates a conflict regarding which party benefits from JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY upon termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14.3.2 In case of termination on the basis of Article 14.1 (b) if IGER is the breaching Party, Article 14.1 (c) if IGER ceases substantially all activities in the COLLABORATION CROPS, Article 14.1 (e) (i) or (ii) or Article 14.1 (f), subject to the obligations of the first sentence of Article 14.3, CERES shall have the exclusive right to use and commercially exploit any JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY to the extent such JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY is not covered by any relevant license/commercialization agreement between the Parties. At or about the effective date of termination, the Parties will negotiate in good faith to reach agreement as to reasonable remuneration (whether as a royalty or in some other form as the Parties may agree), which will be addressed in one or more written agreements. If the Parties fail to reach agreement within ninety (90) days after the start of such negotiations, which shall be evidenced by written notice from one Party to the other initiating such negotiations, the remuneration shall be settled in accordance with the dispute resolution procedure in Article 15.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulates that, under specific termination conditions, each party retains a non-exclusive right to use and commercially exploit JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY. However, the changed text grants CERES an exclusive right to the same JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY under the same termination conditions. This directly contradicts the initial provision, creating an in-text contradiction as it is unclear which party benefits from the property. This is a direct conflict as the Contradicted Text section 14.3.2 also grants CERES exclusive rights to JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY under different conditions, which now overlaps with 14.3.1.
